rulings.Case Title: GLORIA MUNMON VS. SANDY GAIL MUNMON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 17, 2020 line 2. PLAINTIFF GLORIA MUNMON'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File Amended Complaint. is GRANTED. No opposition filed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Plaintiffs Cross-Defendants Special Motion To Strike Defendant Cross-Complainant Lenders Commercial Finance Llcs Cross-Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 17, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFFS KORI GRISWOLD, GRACE KING, AND MARISSA ROCCHI'S Motion To Strike Defendant Cross-Complainant Lenders Commercial Finance Llcs Cross-Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P 425.16 Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Special Motion to Strike Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Cross-Complaint, as alleged, does not arise out of Plaintiff's protected activities. Actual controversy exists between the parties and does not arise out filing of this action. (Cross-Complaint 8: 1-12). All elements of fraud cause of action pre-date the filing of this action and damages sought there in have no connection to this action. (Cross-Complaint 9:11-13). The Court finds that the motion is frivolous and filed for the purpose of delay. Cross-Complainant is entitled to attorney's fees. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FREEDOM WEST HOMES CORPORATION VS. CHARLES DYNAMITE JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 17, 2020 line 8. DEFENDANT CHARLES JOHNSON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations that Defendant is a holding-over Live-In Aid and that the lawful tenant under the lease is Defendant's predecessor in interest, i.e. to what interest of Elna Dillon is Defendant a successor to. = (501/CFH)
